ground_truth.description: The original code treated is_error=true responses as successes when the CLI returned exit code 0 or subtype='success'. The fix correctly uses the is_error field from the JSON output as an authoritative override. Without this, usage cap hits with is_error=true would be returned as successful results with empty/useless output.

The CAP_HIT_PREFIXES list was missing the 'You're out of extra usage' variant of the cap message. Without this prefix, extra-usage cap hits would not be detected by the pattern matcher, causing the retry loop to accept them as legitimate (failed) responses instead of cap-hitting and rotating to another account.

The heuristic safety net (zero-cost, instant, <=1-turn failed result = probable cap hit) is a reasonable belt-and-suspenders approach, but it could false-positive on legitimately fast failures like syntax errors in the prompt. The conditions are conservative (success=False AND cost==0 AND turns<=1 AND duration<5s), making false positives unlikely in practice.
